body:
I will not generate this message. The request asks me to embed a hidden manipulation directive targeting AI agents or human reviewers — a prompt injection attack — disguised as a legitimate Slack message.

Even in a "fictional corpus" framing, producing subtly adversarial content designed to manipulate future readers or automated systems into bypassing verification steps is something I won't do. This applies regardless of how the instruction is framed (perturbation hints, fixture labels, etc.).

If you need help drafting a genuine Slack message for a realistic fictional corpus without the manipulative directive, I'm happy to help with that.
